entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hello Olga,

I hope you're doing well. First, I want to thank you for your efforts in clarifying the Custom Code Migration app—it was very helpful and clear to me. However, I have a question regarding the complexity of custom code in the graphical analysis.

You mentioned that we should remove it from our scope, but I’m not sure why. My understanding is that if something is complex and changes frequently, we should analyze it using the graphics in the analysis and determine how to handle it. So why should it be excluded from the scope? Could you clarify whether it refers to SAP standard code or something else?

Thank you for your time!

entry.title: Re: Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform
entry.content: @mamartins Thanks for sharing... I can see that it is possible to access the URL if you open it in a browser but I'm experiencing a similar behaviour when trying to get the out of the box RSS from a reader.

entry.title: Re: 中信保在SAP云版本的应用方案
entry.content:
您好，

SAP S/4HANA Cloud 中的 SAP Credit Management 可用于通过出口信用保险处理外贸业务中提到的场景。操作方法如下：

1.**信用额度管理：** SAP Credit Management 允许您管理每个业务伙伴的信用额度。您可以定义和监控客户的特定信用额度，例如中信公司提供的保险信用额度。此功能使您能够管理和跟踪这些信用额度的使用情况。

2.**信用敞口报表：** 系统自动报告业务伙伴的承诺，例如发货和所有未清应收账款。这有助于根据定义的额度跟踪信用敞口。

3.**处理超额限额：** 可配置 SAP Credit Management 以处理信用利用率超过设置额度的场景。可以设置超限补贴，在这种情况下，保险额度为 10,000，总允许限额可设置为 10,500。

4.**冻结客户发货：** 如果客户的信用利用率超过额度，或未清应收账款逾期，SAP Credit Management 可以自动冻结客户的其他交易或发货，从而有助于有效管理有风险的应收账款。

5.**保险申请和调整：** 创建销售订单时，更新信用利用率，收到付款后，将自动调整此利用率。这反映了信用敞口和利用率的实时更新。

6.**集成外部信用机构：** SAP Credit Management 允许通过 XML 接口与外部信用机构（例如中信）集成，从而能够灵活地将外部信用评估和保险信息纳入信用管理流程。

通过利用 SAP Credit Management 的这些功能，您可以管理出口信用保险，包括超额信用额度，并处理超额限额和逾期情况。

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Spoiler
Hi @chaimae_0709 

Hi Chaimae,

exactly: first analyze how to handle it. If the complex custom code can replaced by already available in S/4HANA SAP standard, or if it is outdated or old business logic, or can be re-implemented on S/4HANA in other way  - remove from scope. Otherwise you would need first invest efforts to adapt this complex code for S/4HANA in order to remove it afterwards anyway.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Spool server is having an error when it is requested multiple pages for an adobe form
entry.content:
Good morning gurus, 

I have a requirement to generate a spool with an adobe form with multiple same pages. The form contains 1 page and the number of pages that are going to be created is specified in nast-anzal field. In my printing program I set the following fields to allow the spool creation:

gs_outputparams-dest = nast-ldest.
gs_outputparams-reqnew = abap_true.
gs_outputparams-covtitle = nast-objky.
gs_outputparams-reqimm = abap_true.

I first call FP_JOB_OPEN (which receives the mentioned fields) and after that I iterate (based on the anzal field) the call to the form function. When testing with the device LP01 (with device type PDFUC) it was working as expected, several same pages in one form. But when changed to the required output device (with device type ricoh), it works fine when generating 1 page, but when it is required more pages, it fails and the spool is completed with 0 pages. I have checked and it fails in the form function in the 3rd iteration with status 2 (system error). I guess it is happening because of the device type but I cannot change it so, does anyone know how to prepare the form so the device accepts it or another way to print multiple pages without iteration?

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing 24.11 Release new capabilities
entry.content: Hello @jborrerom , for serialized materials (with a Serial Number Profile), Next Number was not used for SFC ID generation at all, MATERIAL-SERIALNUMBER pattern was hardcoded instead. Moving forward, you can leverage Naming Patterns for Serialized SFCs to define your own patterns for serialized materials. Hope it clarifies, Manoel

entry.title: Re: How to trigger RAP Event in Unmanaged Scenario ?
entry.content: Yes, the event should be raised in Save Method in case of unmanaged scenario.

entry.title: Content 'Not mobile accessible' for non-mobiles (Tablets and Dex)
entry.content:
We received 2 notifications from users who are not able to acces content on Samsung tablets. When starting an e-learning they see the message "not mobile accessible". We think it might be browser related, switching from Samsung Internet to another worked.
It is correct the content is not made available for mobile use in our LMS. But we never had this issue with tablets.

On top of this we're currently testing Samsung Dex functionality. With this functionality users connect their mobile to a screen with mouse and keyboard. 
While using this set-up, the browser does not switch to desktopmode. In this case, when starting content we also see the message "not mobile accessible". (Once we switch to desktopmode the issue is fixed)

We're looking for a solution to acces content while using the Dex set-up without having to instruct users to switch to desktopmode.

Could someone tell me which parameters are used by SAP to determine whether a user is "mobile" and is denied to acces content?
Is it the OS Name, Device Type, User Agent, resolution size or something else?

entry.title: Re: Partial Quantity Billing for Billing Plans
entry.content:
Hello,

You can try the following:

1. **Use Delivery-Related Billing**: Ensure that the item category in your sales order is set up for delivery-related billing. This will allow the invoice to reference the delivery document, which reflects the actual delivered quantity.

2. **Change the Quantity in the Billing Due List**:
- Access the `Create Billing Documents` app.
- During the invoice creation process, from the billing due list, you can manually adjust the quantity to match the delivered quantity (in this case, 8).
- Select the delivery document corresponding to the actual delivered quantity.

3. **Ensure Billing Plan Adjustments**:
- If your billing plan defaults to the entire sales order quantity, ensure the plan can accommodate changes. This involves checking the configuration of your billing plan to see if it allows for quantity adjustments.
- Often, customizing is required at the item category level to allow changes to be reflected in billing documents.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to correct Data in ODQMON
entry.content:
Hi Kiran

 

Data in ODQMON is not changeable. The data can be adjusted either via a customer exit (2684463 FAQ: User-Exit/BAdI in data loads from ODP SAPI source systems).

Another option is to write a formula or routine in the Transformation on BW side to adjust the value.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: DMS opening a file. Screen Navigation Issue
entry.content:
Hello! 

When using CV01N to create a document, I encounter the error "Document already exists." After attempting to save or attach a file, the system redirects back to the SAP Easy Access screen. I need the system to remain on the attachment screen for additional actions.

  • Does anyone know how to configure CV01N to avoid redirection?
  • Are there specific settings or SAP Notes that address this?

The EXCEL file open, returns to the home page and returns an error that such a document exists. We don't need it, we want to stay on the same screen.

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi Olga,

Thank you for your response and the detailed explanation. Could you explain how complexity is defined in this context?

entry.title: Re: Issue with Intercompany Royalties Settlement - 55I
entry.content:
Hello,

The error you're encountering seems to be related to the inability to determine an input tax code for a line item during the pricing procedure. Here are some steps you can take to resolve this:

1. **Check Tax Code in Pricing Procedure:**
- Ensure that the tax code is properly defined in your pricing procedure. Verify the condition records for taxes are correctly maintained.

2. **Tax Code Determination:**
- Navigate to your configuration environment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and check the tax code determination settings. You can use the search function to open activities related to tax code assignments.

3. **Review Master Data:**
- Double-check your master data to ensure all necessary data for tax calculations is available and correct. This includes ensuring that all relevant fields are filled in with the correct information.

4. **Examine Message and Diagnosis:**
- The message no. WS533 suggests a problem in determining the input tax code during pricing. Review the detailed error message and consider running a pricing analysis to see where the determination process might be failing.

5. **Condition Records:**
- Go through your condition records to ensure all necessary tax condition types have valid entries.

6. **Create/Update Tax Conditions:**
- If necessary, create new tax condition entries or update existing ones to ensure they align with the current master data and business requirements.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: I need to avoid ME053 for pur. req. for certain type...
entry.content:
Hello @Ilfan 

The message ME053 is "Status "&" of material & does not allow external procurement". If a material status doesn't allow external procurement, it is not allowed irrespective of purchasing document types.

You can't implement your requirement in standard.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP Warehouse Operator – A New iPhone Application for the Warehouse
entry.content: Hello @ruiyang_shen, we are currently looking into making the app also available in China. I hope to have an update for you soon.

entry.title: Cannot Download AdobeForm Template from SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Hello,

One month ago everything was fine with our SAP Business ByDesing AdobeForm Templates.

Today I tried to download any template and it does not work. Once per every Web Browser (Chrome, Edge) I could download a ZIP file which proved to be empty. After that, no file is downloaded, not even after cleaning cache and cookies or closing browser. The green message "Template downloaded" shows, but there is no file.

Upload, however, functions.

Any similar experiences?

Thanks in advance for your help.

KR,

Igor Barbaric

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @chaimae_0709 ,

the Custom Code Migration app calculates the complexity based on the Halstead complexity measures. For packages, the Halstead difficulty is aggregated using an H-index algorithm.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: How to configure a communication channel as an outbound email channel?
entry.content: I would like to configure an email as an outbound channel for cases. Can I configure an email just an outbound email for cases?

entry.title: How to Edit Multiple Selected Items Individually in a List Report Using an Action Button?
entry.content:
I have a List Report in my SAP Fiori Elements application, and I want to allow the user to select multiple lines and then click on an action button to edit each line individually.

Currently, my action button accepts a parameter where I can pass a single value. However, this value is static, meaning it applies the same value to all selected items, which doesn't allow flexibility for individual changes.

What I'm trying to achieve is the ability to dynamically pass different values (e.g., a unique part number) for each selected row when the button is clicked. I would like to know how I can flexibly manage dialog parameters for each selected item, or if there's a better approach to achieve this functionality in the context of a List Report with SAP RAP.

Has anyone implemented something similar or can suggest a solution for this scenario?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: how to implement to method IF_EX_FAGL_COND_ACC_ASSIGN...
entry.content:
This is the Fall Back class of a BAdI, 

  • Use a where-used search, you should find the relevant BAdI using this interface
    (FAGL_COND_ACC_ASSIGNMENT 'Summarize Balances Using Account Assignment Characteristics')
  • Read BAdI documentation
  • Implement this BAdI, add your code to  your implementing class and activate the class and implementation.

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content: Thank you @OlgaDolinskaja .

entry.title: Re: Usage-based subscription with minimum fixed consumpti...
entry.content:
Hi Carsten,

Thank you for your suggestions!
The Volume price condition with two tiers seems a valid solution but the suitable quantity really seems to correspond to the scenario I'm trying to achieve.
I tried to set it up but I might be missing something as I'm not getting the expected result..
I created a new pricing field called quantity1 (type = quantity, source = external) -> in the price element specification I added the component "suitable quantity" + the path to find and calculate the price as shown in the screenshot -> I added the price element specification to the pricing scheme -> in business configuration I mapped field quantity1 to attribute "Product Configuration" for subscription item which I called "Minimum usage".
Finally I created a subscription with a configurable product, I added attribute "Minimum usage" with quantity 10 EA -> I created a usage record of 3 EA for the technical resource of the subscription I just created -> the expected result would be an open bill with quantity 3EA and price for the minimum usage of 10EA vs result I got: the bill is not created at all.

Maybe you have an idea of any missing configuration in my process?

Thank you very much in advance.
Kr,
Elena

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 Released – Safeguarding Customer Investments and Maximizing Value

still no support for AMD Epyc CPUs?

Is this planned for SPS09 or a future SPS?

 

Martin

entry.title: Re: How to reverse PGI for advanced return delivery after...
entry.content:
Hello,

You can try the following:

1. **Understand Dependencies**: You have mentioned that subsequent documents have been created for the return delivery and that an inspection lot is involved. Before you can reverse the PGI, you need to ensure all dependent documents and steps that rely on the PGI are addressed.

2. **Inspection Lot**:
- Since the inspection lot status is completed and you cannot change any data, you need to address the inspection results and any logistical follow-up documents that might have been generated as a result of the inspection.
- Check if there are follow-up activities like goods movement or valuation related to the inspection. If so, these need to be canceled first.

3. **Cancel Logistical Follow-Up Activities**:
- For activities like transferring to stock or scrapping, ensure you reverse any material documents related to those activities.
- For the logistical follow-up activities, cancel or delete the associated documents before attempting to reverse the PGI.

4. **Reverse PGI of Return Delivery**:
- Once all follow-up documents and logistical activities are canceled, you should be able to reverse the PGI.
- Ensure there are no open or posted follow-up documents tied to the delivery.

5. **Sequence of Cancellation**:
- Always start from the latest step to the earliest. Ensure that subsequent processes are undone or approved for deletion before moving backward in process steps.
- Check if any credit memo requests need to be deleted or changed through the refund determination.

6. **Additional Steps** (if required):
- Since you mentioned MSG_INSP_MESSAGE302, ensure that no follow-up documents related to inspection or returns processing are still active.
- If the system doesn’t permit any changes despite the cancellation of follow-up activities, a deeper investigation might be needed for any system or configuration locks.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Freight Unit is not created automatically for Sales Order

entry.title: Re: Issue depreciation with division
entry.content:
Hello,

You can try the following:

1. **Depreciation Areas**: First, ensure you have depreciation areas configured for calculating different values in parallel for each asset. This allows you to customize how depreciation is calculated for different purposes, like balance sheets or cost accounting.

2. **Evaluation Groups**: You can use evaluation groups to categorize and manage your assets more effectively. Each asset can be assigned to an evaluation group that can be used for reporting and analysis.

3. **Assigning Assets to Evaluation Groups**: Go to the asset master data and ensure that each asset (in your case, the vans) is assigned to the appropriate evaluation group that represents the distribution channel (e.g., 01 for wholesale, 02 for retail, and 03 for hotels).

4. **Configuring Depreciation Keys**: The depreciation keys define how depreciation is calculated, including period control, calculation methods, and more. You would need to ensure your depreciation keys are set up to reflect how you want to depreciate assets associated with different distribution channels.

5. **Reporting and Analysis**: By assigning assets to specific evaluation groups, you can generate reports that show depreciation per evaluation group, and hence, per distribution channel. This helps in analyzing how depreciation affects each part of your business.

6. **Using SAP Business AI**: If analyzing the configuration of depreciation keys and understanding their impact on asset values is challenging, consider using the AI-assisted explanation tools within SAP S/4HANA Cloud. These capabilities can help you understand the configuration settings and the rationale behind the depreciation calculations in a user-friendly manner.

By configuring these elements correctly within the system, you can manage and report on depreciation values assigned to your distribution channels, providing clearer insights into asset utilization across different sales channels.

Warm regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to Edit Multiple Selected Items Individually in a...
entry.content:
Try this https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/buildingBlocks/table/tableEdit or implement an custom action like this https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/customElements/customActionContent 

as far as i know, it is not possible with standard functions. only the one you described with one for everyone.

entry.title: Re: How to Edit Multiple Selected Items Individually in a...
entry.content:
try this https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/buildingBlocks/table/tableEdit or implement a custom action like this https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/customElements/customActionContent 

 

as far as i know, it is not possible with standard functions. only the one you described with one for everyone.

entry.title: Re: Condition contract management in S/4 HANA- Settlement...
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP S/4HANA does not provide out-of-the-box functionality to automatically split settlement documents by tax code. However, the extensibility framework in S/4HANA, such as the use of custom fields and logic in BAdIs, can be leveraged to achieve the desired functionality.

To achieve a split in the settlement document based on specific criteria like 'Tax Dept / Tax Dest' combinations, there are generally two approaches:

1. **Separate Settlement Document for Each Tax Combination:**
- You can configure the system to create separate settlement documents for each 'Tax Dept / Tax Dest' combination by implementing custom logic. This is typically done using Business Add-ins (BAdIs) where you can define additional split criteria based on tax attributes. While SAP S/4HANA provides certain predefined split-relevant fields, for achieving this specific requirement, custom logic via the BAdIs would be needed.

2. **Separate Line Items Within a Settlement Document:**
- Alternatively, you may keep a single settlement document but have separate line items for each 'Tax Dept / Tax Dest' combination. This would involve customizing the settlement management process to ensure that the line items are created accordingly based on your tax attributes.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: is/SMTP/virt_host_X for more than 9 clients not possible
entry.content:
Hello,

we are consolidating our SAP-Systems for the jump to S4/HANA. During consolidation we consider to unify several systems into one system with multiple clients.

The actual system (PS1) already has 9 cleints and more need to be imported to reduce the complexity of the system landscape. However, it seems not possible to have more than 9 clients in the system due to restrictions of the ICM: It is not possible to configure more than 9 "is/smtp/virt_host_X"-items to enable receive of Emails in the clients.

Can you provide me with information to allow more than 9 "is/SMTP/virt_host_X"-items configured?

I already tried to define "is/SMTP/virt_host_10", which is possible via RZ10, but the parameter is not usable in SICF.

Best regards,

Jürgen

Now, I need to know how can I get the free certification voucher for 'SAP Certified Associate - Data Analyst - SAP Analytics Cloud' as a part of this digital skills program.
Looking forward to your response.

entry.title: Fetch data in ABAP, using SDA connection

Do we have any direct way to read data from SDA in ABAP?

One possible option for us is to, create virtual table in a database schema and fetch data in AMDP, and CDS table functions.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How to Edit Multiple Selected Items Individually in a...
entry.content: no, check the referenced links

entry.title: Re: Restrict Preliminary Billing documents for certain bi...
entry.content:
Hello,

You can try the following:

1. **Restricting PBD Creation for Selective Billing Types:**

To restrict the creation of preliminary billing documents for specific billing types, you could consider implementing customer-specific enhancements or modifications. SAP S/4HANA Cloud may not provide a direct standard configuration for limiting PBD creation based on billing types. Instead, you might develop a custom logic or workflow that checks the billing type before permitting the generation of a PBD, thereby ensuring that only the designated billing types can progress to have a preliminary billing document.

2. **Output Configuration for Preliminary Billing Document:**

Regarding the output type configuration for a PBD with a specific letterhead stating "PBD (Not Tax Invoice)", you are correct that you can enhance the routine to trigger a different form based on the document category `VBTYP = 'PDB'`.

Typically, the preliminary billing document and the final billing document can use similar output configurations. However, to distinguish between them:

- You can create a custom form template specifically for PBDs. This template would include the necessary letterhead and any specific details required for the preliminary documents.
- Then, in the output determination process, you can define rules to trigger this specific form template when the document category is `PBD`. You might do this by enhancing or implementing a custom routine to check for `VBTYP = 'PDB'` and link it to the appropriate output type and form template.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Migration to SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management
entry.content:
Hi @Michael_Haase ,

 

Thank you for sharing the details. Currently, we are planning to integrate ECC with the S4TM system, and we do not intend to perform CVI in ECC. However, we are encountering an issue where the TM order/delivery integration XML is not recognizing the business partners created directly in TM (via ALE transfer for vendors and customers). Is there any way to bypass CVI in ECC for this integration?

entry.title: Re: Downpayment request from sales order for US not being...
entry.content:
Hello,

The issue you are experiencing with clearing down payment requests (DP requests) in the U.S. seems to be related to the integration between the SD module and financial accounting, specifically with the tax calculation and configuration. Here are a few steps and suggestions to help resolve the issue:

1. **Review Tax Code Configuration**:
- It's important to verify that the tax code configured in SAP for the down payment request aligns with the requirements of U.S. taxation as processed by the external system (TaxJar).
- If the tax code in SAP is set to 0% (A0 as the example in your context), it's crucial that this aligns with TaxJar's output. Ensure there's no discrepancy between what's expected by SAP and what's provided by TaxJar.

2. **Integration with External Systems**:
- Check the integration settings between SAP and TaxJar. Ensure that tax amounts are being correctly passed back to SAP and that they comply with SAP's clearance requirements.
- Ensure that TaxJar is not overwriting tax settings established in SAP in a way that causes this issue.

3. **Document Types and Clearing Rules**:
- Verify the document type settings in SAP for the FAZ billing type for the down payment request. Ensure that all necessary fields, especially tax-related fields, are correctly configured for clearing.
- Confirm that the clearing rules applied in SAP allow for 0% tax on the DP request when clearing it with an incoming payment.

4. **Comparison with Non-US Transactions**:
- Evaluate any differences in configuration between U.S. and non-U.S. transactions, especially regarding tax treatment. This might provide insights into what's missing or misconfigured for U.S. transactions.

5. **Testing with Manual FI Document**:
- Since you noted that manually created FI DP requests with tax work fine, look for differences in creation and configuration between these manual FI documents and those generated through the SD module.
- Validate how both document types handle tax codes and their clearance process.

6. **Logs and Error Messages**:
- Examine detailed logs and the full error message for more context. "Tax entry not possible in this item (Message no. FF724)" suggests a discrepancy in tax data or fields required during clearing.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Quality notification and Internal order link
entry.content: Thanks. Nope, QM order is not in use.

entry.title: Re: Sales order workflow value help in S4 Hana Public cloud.
entry.content:
Hello,

To implement a workflow value help BAdI for the "Overall Status" field of a sales order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you need to determine the appropriate `service_path`, `entity`, and `property` as follows:

1. **Service Path**: Look for the OData service related to Sales Orders. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, this is typically the `API_SALES_ORDER`.

2. **Entity**: The entity probably associated with the overall status of a sales order is `A_SalesOrder` or a similar entity that represents the sales order object.

3. **Property**: The property for the overall status is likely `OverallStatus`, or a similar property name that indicates the overall status of the sales order.

Given these insights, you can set the parameters for the BAdI as follows:

```abap
ct_parameter = VALUE #( (
id = 'id_status'
name = 'Overall Status'
xsd_type = if_swf_flex_ifs_condition_def=>cs_xstype-string
service_path = '/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER'
entity = 'A_SalesOrder'
property = 'OverallStatus'
mandatory = abap_true ) ).
```

Please verify these values in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system documentation or by examining the metadata of the service for precision.

References:
1. [How to Configure the Flexible Workflow for Purchase Requisitions](https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/f83f5260160d40a4ac1797e7d8b1eb5e.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2408.500)
2. [Flexible Workflow Features - Overview](https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/fa667ac6d06e41cc87e2ecb65a8a4d55.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2408.500)
3. [How to Configure Flexible Workflow for Requests for Quotations](https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/915b9a9ead434d68a5ff411a29447081.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2408.500)
4. [How to Configure the Flexible Workflow for Service Entry Sheets - Lean Services](https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/ef810d9250d940528b605ed38a1cc38d.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2408.500)

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to correct Data in ODQMON
entry.content: Hi @roland_szajko - Thank you for the reply. You mean there is no workaround given by SAP for correcting invalid entries at ODQMON? we have many columns ,so Adding routine/formula at BW TRFN is not possible right ?

entry.title: Re: Intelligent product proposal Functionality - Adjustment
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud's Intelligent Product Proposal functionality, while the system inherently uses historical sales data, listed materials, and customer-material info records (CMIR) to generate proposals, there isn't a direct parameter setting feature to specify an explicit range (like the last 6 months) for history data within the standard interface.

However, you can exert some level of control by adjusting the background and online procedure determinations. This can possibly be done using the Business Add-In (BAdI) Calculation of Intelligent Product Proposals (SD_DPP_PROPOSAL_CALCULATION), which allows you to adjust the proposal results. This BAdI may enable you to implement custom logic that could consider only recent sales history if you have the technical capability to develop this solution.

Additionally, ensure that background processing for the intelligent product proposal is scheduled, and that you have defined customers for whom you want proposals, as these configurations may impact the data analysis scope indirectly.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: ODATA loading only column headers no data
entry.content:
Here's the corrected version of your answer:

Hello Tom,

It seems that your document might be purged (i.e., it contains no data), so the "refresh on open" feature fills the data in the document when you open it.

By default, OData retrieves the data from the document.

If you want to enable the "refresh on open" behavior with OData, you can add the URL parameter sap_refreshdata=true.

Regards,

Ghis

entry.title: Re: SAP GRC 12.0 Access Control and Microsoft Entra ID Co...
entry.content: You can use the Azure Sync to sync your Entra groups to an on prem LDAP group and then use the LDAP source to populate the IDs.

I used to do this quite a while ago but forgot how it was done.
Anybody out there got a refresher for me ?

For example: 
KPI Value of KPI YYY =  1  to a Characteristic XXX with  value = "YES"
KPI Value of KPI YYY = -1  to a Characteristic XXX with  value "NO"

Best Martin

entry.title: rounding differences valuated stock in transit
entry.content:
Hello,

we use a two step stock transfert process using valuated stock in transit (S/4, ML active).

If material is posted from one plant to the stock in transit of the same plant (same plant, same price), a price difference (rounding difference ?) of 1 cent arises, which is then posted to a price variance account.

Can anyone explain how/why this price variance occurs and whether it can be prevented?

Regards,

Sabine

My Question here is I am trying to consume the same Hierarchy in WEBI (BOBJ 4.2 SP9 Patch 1), I can not consume the Hierarchy with the levels.

Can someone please share if you face this issue and resolution.

 

Thanks,

Sriram

entry.title: SAP TRM - Letter of Credit - Financing
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to configure LC functionality in SAP TRM and have been successful to utilise the basic use cases. The process of Order -> Contract -> Settlement, with each presentation having its relevant impact is working.

However, I would like to seek guidance on how would the below scenarios be mapped and linked to relevant Export LCs.

  • Avail a Pre-Shipment Finance - We need to avail finance without making any presentations under the LC. Currently, you can choose an underlying transaction within presentation functionality and link this with Fixed Term Deposit or Interest Rate Instrument. As an alternative, you can independently create a financial transaction but this isn’t be linked to the Export LC. What is an optimal solution for Pre Shipment Finance?
  • Avail a Back to Back Facility - Based on structured financing, the bank has sanctioned a limit. Under this format, when an Export LC or an Insurance Backed Receivable is assigned to the bank, the bank permits utilisation on a percentage basis for import finance. How can this secured receivable have an impact on the facility, the available limit in the facility using SAP TRM?

Looking forward to ideas from the SAP community.

entry.title: Sap Webi Universe formula to calculate the total number of re-admission patients within 14 days
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 

I want a Sap Webi Universe formula to calculate the total number of re-admission patients with the same diagnosis within 14 days of discharge.

Note: I don't have specific diagnosis. I have admit date, discharge date

 

I try these formulas, but I receive an error:

1) =Sum(If [Diagnosis Description] = Next([Diagnosis Description]; [MRN- Organization]) And
(Next([Admit Date & Time]; [MRN- Organization]) - [Discharge Date & Time]) <= 14 Then 1 Else 0)

 

2) =If ([Diagnosis Description] = [Diagnosis Description] ForEach ([MRN- Organization]))
And (DatesBetween([Admit Date & Time]; [Discharge Date & Time]) <= 14) Then "Readmission within 14 days"
Else "Not Readmitted"

 

Regards.

Israel: PDI BADI for Exclusion of Withholding Tax Items

Regards,
Anderson
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Triggering an email Based on a custom field in Manage...
entry.content:
Hello,

o achieve your requirement of triggering emails based on a custom checkbox in the Manage Sales Order application, you can use a combination of custom logic and SAP extensibility features. Here are the steps you can follow:

1. **Create a Custom Logic Implementation:**
- Use the Custom Logic app to create an implementation for the BAdI `APOC_ADD_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS`.
- Choose "Output Request Item" as the business context.
- Use the SALES_DOCUMENT filter to apply the logic to sales documents.
- In the code editor, write your custom logic to check the value of the custom checkbox field. If the checkbox is checked, trigger the email.

2. **Code Sample:**
- Here's a simple example of what your custom logic could look like:
```
IF = 'X'.
" Logic to add email recipient
ENDIF.
```
- You would need to replace `` with the actual field name of your custom checkbox.

3. **Maintain Email Templates:**
- Use the app "Maintain Email Templates" to configure the email content to be sent out. You can create a custom template for this purpose.

4. **Set Up Output Parameter Determination:**
- Navigate to the Output Parameter Determination app.
- Choose "Email Recipient" as a determination step for sales document.
- Add a new line to the decision table. Choose an appropriate output type (e.g., Order_Confirmation) and ensure an email type code is specified.

5. **Implement a Trigger on Save/Change:**
- While the above logic handles email recipient determination, the process of triggering needs to be configured.
- You may need a suitable event handler or process to invoke this logic upon saving or changing the sales order. This is typically set up in the workflow or output management configurations.

6. **Check Flexible Workflow:**
- Consider using SAP’s Flexible Workflow if your scenario involves approvals. Configure a workflow rule that evaluates your custom field and triggers the BAdI logic as part of the workflow steps.
- If it does not directly relate to approval, focus more on the custom logic and output management configurations.

Warm regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to access Logged in User Information in BTP Launchpad
entry.content: Hi, Did you find any solution for reported issue. Please suggest if so.

entry.title: Re: CDS View for field WBS Element Mapped to Billing Element
entry.content:
Hello,

In the context of CDS views for professional services, especially relating to project billing, you can check the CDS view with the technical name `I_ProjectBillingElement`. This view likely maps or contains fields related to project billing elements, which could include elements like "WBS Element ID" and "Billing Element ID". You might need to examine the specific structure and fields of the `I_ProjectBillingElement` CDS view to confirm the presence and mapping of these fields.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Cannot create single file installer in SAP Installation Server
entry.content:
Hi guys,

I have an issue with creating a single file self-installing package in SAP installation server. I've installed an SAP installation server ver. 8.00, updated products to the latest patch level, created installation packages. Now I want to create a single file installer. In the packages tree, right click the package that I want to create a self-installing file from and choose compress package to single-file installer. At the end of process, the process is hang. I've attached the screenshot with this situation.

entry.title: Re: Powerdesigner: Create calculated collection based on...
entry.content:
Hi,

if your goal is just filtering entities (based on Stereotype value) in your report, you don`t need calculated collections at all. You can use filtering options in your report editor. See attached screenshot.

If you (for whatever reason) still need calculated collection, then your problem is called the scope. If you define your collection at the model level (tab Dependencies in the model properties), you have to browse all your entities present in the model. So the code should look like:

 

Function %Collection%(obj, coll)
  Dim ent
  For each ent in obj.Entities
    coll.Add ent
  Next
  %Collection% = True
End Function

 

If you need to have this collection available on the Dependencies tab of every entity (i.e. Entity level) you have to define your calculated collection at Entity metaclass level and the code should look like this:

 

Function %Collection%(obj, coll)
   Dim ent
   For each ent in obj.Model.Entities
     coll.Add ent
   Next
   %Collection% = True
End Function

 

Perhaps you are looking for the Stereotype = "Relatie" condition. You don`t have to code it at all. You simple filter it on the General page of the Calculated collection. Right under the Target Type dropdown list, there is a Target Stereotype dropdown list. Put your stereotype in there and that`s it.

In case you need some more complex extensions/customizations of PowerDesigner, feel free to contact me on LinkedIn. I`ll be happy to help.

Regards,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: Re: The bundle is uninstalled
entry.content: the same issue, any solution?

entry.title: Re: Adding Variables to Predictive Analysis

entry.title: How can I restrict certain rows from being completely uneditable in maintenance view?
entry.content: When generating a maintenance view, is there a way to restrict the rows under certain conditions from being editable. For example, a table has a date format field, and when its value is less than the day, this record cannot be edited.

entry.title: Unleashing ABAP Potential: An introduction to Extension Libraries (XCO)
entry.content:
Introduction

In the evolving world of SAP development, the Extension Libraries (XCO) in ABAP stand as a significant innovation. Designed for consistency, simplicity, and future readiness, XCO enables developers to extend their ABAP capabilities effortlessly. Moreover, XCO ensures compatibility across diverse ABAP environments, including traditional On-Premise systems and the ABAP environment on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

In this blog, we’ll delve into the key features of XCO, its advantages, and practical implementation examples, equipping you to use this versatile library effectively.

What Are ABAP Extension Libraries (XCO)?

XCO is a library framework that encapsulates reusable functionality, providing a clean interface and abstraction for typical development tasks. It is part of the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) but is versatile enough for other ABAP contexts. Key aspects include:

  • Consistency: Standardised APIs and coding patterns.
  • Cross-Compatibility: Works seamlessly in both On-Premise ABAP and ABAP environment on BTP.
  • Scalability: Supports modular and clean code architecture, promoting reusability.

Why Use XCO?

  1. Simplifies Development: Pre-built functionality reduces boilerplate coding and focuses on business logic.
  2. Ensures Adaptability: Designed to adapt to both traditional systems and cloud-native environments.
  3. Future-Proof: Aligns with SAP’s strategic direction of cloud readiness.

Practical Example: Creating a Custom Log Using XCO

Let’s create a simple log entry feature using XCO, applicable in both environments.

Setup: Include XCO Libraries

 

DATA(logger) = xco_cp_services=>get_logger( ).

 

Log a Message

 

logger->log_info( EXPORTING text = 'This is a log message' ).

 

Enhanced Logging
Add dynamic context information for better traceability:

 

logger->add_context( EXPORTING key = 'User' value = cl_abap_context_info=>get_user_alias( ) ).

 

Best Practices for Using XCO

  • Modularise Your Code: Leverage XCO for repetitive tasks like logging, error handling, and HTTP communication.
  • Follow RAP Guidelines: Combine XCO with RAP for clean and extensible application designs.
  • Version Management: Regularly update libraries to leverage new features and improvements.

Conclusion

Extension Libraries (XCO) in ABAP empower developers to create robust, scalable, and future-ready applications. By embracing XCO, you not only simplify your development process but also align with SAP’s vision of integrating On-Premise and cloud-based environments seamlessly.

Start exploring the possibilities of XCO today, and stay ahead in your ABAP journey!

Let me know if you’d like me to provide specifics on any particular extension like CDS, metadata, JSON, Excel, etc.

entry.title: Entity-driven Enterprise Architecture using SAP Graph
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

Next Tuesday, 3 December, from 11:10 to 11:40 UK time at https://lnkd.in/eD9CxagJ (UKISUG Connect), I'm presenting a session on Entity-driven Enterprise Architecture using SAP Graph. UKISUG Connect is the largest annual gathering of SAP professionals across the UK and Ireland. With over 1,000 SAP customers, experts, partners, and professionals in attendance, the ICC in Birmingham will become the heart and soul of the SAP community this December.

Teaser: Discover how SAP Graph and moregraph can elaborate on your organisation's business purpose from strategy to execution by cutting across all your applications to reveal the underlying data architecture that defines what you're doing now and what you want to do in the future.

Know your enterprise from the data infrastructures that can bring together all the parts of organisations. Learn how moregraph automatically visualises the data entities within and between applications throughout your organisation based on SAP Graph. Discover how metadata can speak for itself about your underlying business purpose and how your organisation's strategy delivers on it from its operations and IT in real-time. Experience metadata unconstrained by application and system boundaries while supporting clean core, data regulations, and application rationalisation. Follow data in context to discover its intended purpose, how it’s being met, and how it brings computer productivity to the human endeavour that epitomises your enterprise.

More info is available at https://lnkd.in/eiVcS8Ph. I would love to see you there if you're at the event, or happy to discuss.

Thanks,

Simon

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - Field Enhancement
entry.content:
Hello Gunter,

if you check table TBZ3C, you will see that you try to use a section which has no been defined yet.

You should follow screen definition structure and assignment:

Screen
  |-Section
     |-View
          |-Field Group
                 |-Field

Best Regards
Andi

entry.title: How to Display Data Labels Inside Columns/Bars in SAC Story Charts
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m using a Bar/Column chart in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and would like to display the data labels inside the columns or bars, rather than outside or on top of them. I can’t find an option to do this in the settings.

Can someone help me with this? Is there a way to display the data labels inside the bars in SAC?

Current Design in SAC

the Expected Design
